atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports pain in the left hand, describing it as a dull ache.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports the left hand pain has been present for the past week.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient rates the left hand pain as 4 out of 10.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient denies any recent trauma.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports intermittent left hand pain.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports left hand pain particularly with prolonged use.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Physical exam reveals mild tenderness to palpation over the distal left wrist.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Range of motion is full and without limitation.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: There is no swelling or erythema.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Neurological exam is intact, with full strength and sensation in the left hand.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient's left hand pain is concerning for possible carpal tunnel syndrome or other musculoskeletal issues.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Order wrist X-ray to evaluate for carpal tunnel syndrome.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Refer to orthopedics for further evaluation if X-ray is abnormal.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Advise patient to avoid repetitive movements and use ergonomic equipment.
